_comment: Academic Studio package catalog. Served at https://academic-studio.com/packages.json; the app fetches it on startup and in Run Setup, so publishing a package = edit this file + run scripts/make-package.sh + push (GitHub Pages deploys site/**). Entries are declarative data (pip names, sha256-pinned skill tarball, MCP config) — the app never executes shell text from this file. After editing a skill's files under packages/<id>/, bump its version here and re-run scripts/make-package.sh <id>.

packages.label: Finance Data — fetch market & economic data (Yahoo Finance, FRED, SEC EDGAR, Ken French, FinnHub, Financial Modeling Prep)
packages.manualSteps: Open a terminal and run: pip install yfinance pandas-datareader finnhub-python requests (if you see an 'externally-managed-environment' error, run PIP_BREAK_SYSTEM_PACKAGES=1 pip install … instead) — then download the skill archive from academic-studio.com/packages and unpack it into ~/.claude/skills/finance-data. FinnHub and Financial Modeling Prep need free API keys, stored as FINNHUB_API_KEY and FMP_API_KEY in your environment (from finnhub.io and financialmodelingprep.com); Claude walks you through this on first use.

packages.label: Slide Deck — build themed reveal.js/Quarto presentations, export to PDF/PPTX/PNG
packages.manualSteps: Download the skill archive from academic-studio.com/packages and unpack it into ~/.claude/skills/slides. Rendering needs Quarto; PDF/PPTX/PNG export needs Node.js and decktape — install both from Help → Run Setup… (Quarto is standard; decktape is the opt-in 'decktape' program).

packages.label: Voiceover Builder — narrate a PDF slide deck into an MP4 video + transcript (Claude Code writes the narration, ElevenLabs voices it)
packages.manualSteps: Download the skill archive from academic-studio.com/packages and unpack it into ~/.claude/skills/voiceover. Rendering the deck needs Quarto and Python — install both from Help → Run Setup…. Set ELEVENLABS_API_KEY in your environment (a free key from elevenlabs.io) for the text-to-speech voice; Claude walks you through this on first use. No other key is needed — the narration is written by Claude Code. The first launch sets up a small local Python environment automatically.
